human_verification.notes: Reviewed Issue #1248 source baseline, industry boundary, scenario families, functionality mapping, and deployment/commercialization posture for colocation operations execution.

specialist_owned_evidence: Manifest defines explicit colocation execution ownership, refusal boundaries, lifecycle state rules, and adjacent routing safeguards.
Authority sources in manifest and research-summary are mapped to workflow stages, artifacts, systems, and decision boundaries.
Scenarios include normal execution, missing-artifact blocking, exception handling, completion refusal, adjacent-lane routing, policy conflict, and evidence rework closure.
Functionality map declares all required shared + execution phase + industry-profile abilities and maps each to passing scenario IDs.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Source refresh and interpretation refinement can be handled by higher-level catalog maintenance when sources or tenant overlays change.
Commercial readiness claims rely on sustained deployment telemetry and issue #1248 completion evidence.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated claim; all ambiguities are non-blocking and require explicit tenant-supplied precedence/retention clarifications.
Execution remains `specification-based`; trusted built promotion requires separate governance-approved package and sustained safe operation telemetry.
